microglial_substates: LDAM (Lipid Droplet-Associated Microglia): ACSL1+/NAMPT+/DPYD+/CD163+ — explicitly distinct from DAM and homeostatic; other microglial states not primary focus
DAM1_markers_confirmed: APOE/TREM2 noted as general DAM markers; LDAM defined by ACSL1/lipid droplet signature (not DAM-1-specific)
DAM2_markers_confirmed: LDAM: ACSL1+/NAMPT+/CD163+ — human-specific lipid-toxic state; TREM2 noted but not a driver of LDAM

notes_for_DAM_DRUG: INDEPENDENT COHORT (Banner Sun Health Research Institute — not ROSMAP). CRITICAL NEW STATE: LDAM (Lipid Droplet-Associated Microglia) = ACSL1+/NAMPT+/DPYD+/CD163+ — a neurotoxic lipid-accumulating microglial state distinct from canonical DAM. Likely corresponds to or overlaps with Gabitto Micro-PVM_4 (lipid-associated) and Sun 2023 MG4 (lipid processing) but not formally mapped. APOE4-DEPENDENCE: LDAM most abundant in APOE4/4 AD; APOE required mechanistically for Aβ-triggered ACSL1/TG synthesis. This mandates APOE4 stratification in DAM-DRUG Phase 1 analysis (APOE4 status is in Gabitto metadata — confirmed feasible). NEUROTOXICITY MECHANISM: LDAM conditioned media → tau phosphorylation (AT8+) + caspase-3-mediated neuronal apoptosis in iPSC-derived neurons. Phagocytosis impaired. DRUGGABLE TARGETS: ACSL1 (Triacin C inhibitor); PIK3CA/PI3K (GNE-317) — both reverse LD accumulation in iPSC-microglia. These are metabolic enzyme targets (not TFs), adding a non-TF arm to DAM-DRUG target space. TF MOTIFS (HOMER/ATAC): PU.1/Sfpi1, REL (NF-κB), ETV6, FOSL2/JUND, RUNX2 enriched in LDAM ATAC peaks — corroborates RELB (Sun 2023) and RUNX2 (Sun 2023) findings. PPARG NOT found — gap: PPARγ is a master lipid metabolism TF validated in Sun 2023 but not identified in Haney ATAC; may reflect different stimulation conditions or detection threshold. PI3K inhibitor GNE-317: note this compound does not cross BBB well — CNS penetrance is a concern for therapeutic translation.

brain_region: Frontal cortex (human IHC + snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics)
neuropath_staging: AD vs. control; disease stage (late AD in mouse: ~3 months 5xFAD)
microglial_substates: PU.1-low CD28+ (neuroprotective suppressive state); conventional inflammatory/IRM state; homeostatic
DAM1_markers_confirmed: PU.1-low subset = CD28+/PD-1+/CTLA-2A+ — lymphoid-like co-stimulatory marker profile; plaque-associated
DAM2_markers_confirmed: PU.1-low CD28+ state is distinct from and suppresses conventional DAM/IRM inflammatory states
TREM2_dependency_assessed: Y — TREM2/CLEC7A → SYK/PLCγ2 signaling drives PU.1 downregulation upon plaque contact
TREM2_dependency_result: PU.1 downregulation is a CONSEQUENCE of plaque contact via TREM2/CLEC7A signaling — not a primary driver; represents a natural neuroprotective microglial response to amyloid
mouse_model_comparison: 5xFAD primary model; human validation by snRNA-seq + spatial + IHC
conservation_across_datasets: Confirmed in human frontal cortex (N=3 IHC); PU.1-low CD28+ subset confirmed present
notes_for_DAM_DRUG: CRITICAL CONFIRMATORY FINDING FOR SPI1/PU.1 INHIBITION STRATEGY: Lowering PU.1 expression in microglia (microglia-specific Cx3cr1-CreErt2 haploinsufficiency + siRNA) → (1) reduced amyloid plaque load; (2) reduced neuroinflammation; (3) improved memory; (4) extended lifespan in 5xFAD mice. This provides the first causal in vivo evidence that SPI1/PU.1 inhibition is therapeutic in AD. MECHANISM: PU.1-low microglia adopt a distinct neuroprotective CD28+/PD-1+ lymphoid-like state that SUPPRESSES conventional IRM/inflammatory microglial states — it is not simply less-active microglia, it is a different protective identity. CD28 KO in microglia → protective state lost → inflammatory IRM state promoted → accelerated plaque growth. THERAPEUTIC IMPLICATIONS: (1) SPI1 INHIBITION confirmed as correct strategy — add to plan as causally validated; (2) CD28 AGONISM added as parallel therapeutic angle — microglial CD28 agonist could promote neuroprotective state without requiring direct TF targeting; (3) PU.1-low state is a NEW microglial substate to annotate in Phase 1 — should be identifiable in SEA-AD + ROSMAP snRNA-seq data as a CD28+/PD-1+ plaque-associated cluster. IMPORTANT DISTINCTION: The PU.1-low state is NOT the same as DAM-1/DAM-2 — it is a third microglial identity that resists CSF1R inhibition. This adds complexity to the microglial landscape: homeostatic → DAM/IRM (inflammatory) AND homeostatic → PU.1-low CD28+ (protective). Therapeutic goal = promote the latter arm. HUMAN VALIDATION: confirmed by snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics + IHC in human frontal cortex (N=3 AD for IHC — small; further human evidence needed). PRIMARY LIMITATION: Mouse-primary study; human N is small (N=3 IHC). Human snRNA-seq N not reported in abstract — verify in Methods.

notes_for_DAM_DRUG: LARGEST HUMAN MICROGLIA DATASET (194K nuclei; 443 ROSMAP donors). COHORT NOTE: ROSMAP biobank — same cohort infrastructure as Mathys 2019 and Zhou 2020 — NOT independent. CRITICAL NEW FINDING 1 — DAM IS NOT A DISCRETE CLUSTER: DAM signatures (Keren-Shaul gene set) are distributed across all 12 microglial states at low levels — the clean DAM-1/DAM-2 bifurcation is an oversimplification of a more heterogeneous/continuous transcriptional landscape in human. CRITICAL NEW FINDING 2 — PPARG AS TIER 1 REPURPOSABLE TARGET: PPARG causally validated to preserve homeostatic phenotype in iPSC-microglia overexpression experiment — morphological rescue + calcium response restoration. PPARG = nuclear receptor (Finan Tier 1; approved agonists: pioglitazone/rosiglitazone) — IMMEDIATELY REPURPOSABLE. Pioglitazone has prior AD clinical trials (TOMMORROW trial). PLAN AMENDMENT TRIGGERED: add PPARG as priority repurposing target alongside TF screens. NEW TF RUNX2: distinct from RUNX1; causal homeostatic rescue validated in iMGLs. INFLAMMATORY BLOCKERS VALIDATED: FOXO3 / HIF1A / FOXP2 knockdown (CRISPRi) completely blocked IL1B induction in LPS-stimulated iMGLs. RELB: GWAS-linked NF-kB family TF driving MG10 inflammatory state. IRF8 NUANCE: IRF8 overexpression did NOT significantly rescue homeostasis in iPSC validation screen (25 TFs tested) — overexpression ≠ homeostatic rescue. This does not invalidate IRF8 INHIBITION as a strategy (different mechanism) but removes IRF8 from the validated homeostatic-rescue TF set. GRN METHOD: DoRothEA + ATAC co-accessibility + Homer motif enrichment — NOT pySCENIC. Does not directly benchmark pySCENIC but provides orthogonal GRN validation. SEX: MG3 proportion significantly differs by sex — additional sex-covariate rationale. MG4 correlation with Braak is the strongest state-pathology association.

total_microglia_N: Not reported separately; subset of 2,263,395 snRNA-seq + 1,217,165 snATAC-seq total nuclei

DAM2_markers_confirmed: DAM signature enriched in Mic TPT1 terminally activated pole; lipid/inflammatory genes enriched; specific DAM-2 markers not broken out

notes_for_DAM_DRUG: LARGEST MULTIOME DATASET IN AD (2.26M RNA + 1.22M ATAC nuclei; 111 ROSMAP donors; 6 brain regions). COHORT NOTE: ROSMAP biobank — same infrastructure as Mathys 2019 / Sun 2023 / Zhou 2020. Together these four ROSMAP papers create a severe non-independence issue: effectively one biobank provides 4 of 6 PICO1 evidence points. CRITICAL NEW CONCEPT — EPIGENOMIC EROSION: In late-stage AD, terminally activated microglia do not represent a stable programmed DAM state — they suffer catastrophic breakdown of nuclear chromatin compartmentalization. Repressive heterochromatin (Polycomb domains / Lamina-Associated Domains) aberrantly opens while active regulatory elements close. TFs enriched in eroded states: IRF8 / MAF / RELA (chromVAR motif analysis). THERAPEUTIC WINDOW IMPLICATION: Epigenomic erosion suggests late-stage DAM may be irreversibly dysfunctional rather than actively regulated. DAM-DRUG therapeutic window should therefore target EARLY stages (before erosion onset = before epigenomic collapse in late AD). This STRENGTHENS the plan rationale for early DAM-1/MG4-early targeting but adds a new framing: the goal is preventing erosion, not reversing it. SCENIC+ RE-EVALUATION: NOT triggered — Liu 2025 did not benchmark GRN accuracy (AUROC) for ATAC vs RNA-only; pySCENIC decision remains closed. ATAC motif data confirms IRF8/MAF/RELA as active in AD microglia (orthogonal validation of existing priority TFs) but does not add new TFs or change priority rankings.

neuropath_staging: AD vs control; APOE/TREM2/APP/PSEN1 variant carriers included in discovery cohort
DAM1_markers_confirmed: Not primary focus — APOE regulatory GRN was primary output
conservation_across_datasets: Replicated in ROSMAP cohort (independent validation of TF assignments; >80% reproducibility threshold across 100 pySCENIC runs required)
notes_for_DAM_DRUG: GRN VALIDATION STUDY — PICO3 RELEVANT. Uses pySCENIC v0.12.1 (RNA only — no ATAC; limitation acknowledged). APOE regulators in human microglia: CEBPA + CEBPB + CEBPD (all microglial states); IRF7 (resting + activated); JUND (resting); JUNB (activated/inflammatory). SPI1 and IKZF1 recovered as pan-state TFs but NOT as direct APOE regulators. IRF8 / RUNX1 / PPARG not identified as APOE regulators. No APOE4 vs APOE3 genotype stratification performed — acknowledged as a limitation. PLAN NOTE: CEBP family (CEBPA/CEBPB/CEBPD) are primary APOE regulators in human microglia across all states — not in current DAM-DRUG TF priority list. Consider adding CEBPB as secondary candidate given pan-state presence and APOE-regulatory role in APOE4-dependent LDAM biology. GRN confidence approach: 100 pySCENIC runs per state; >80% reproducibility threshold; ROSMAP replication — robust validation methodology applicable to DAM-DRUG Phase 2. Preprint status (medRxiv 2024) — treat as evidence_tier 3 until peer review.

DAM1_markers_confirmed: Lipid clearance / efferocytosis / lysosomal processing genes — LAM-relevant (cholesterol efflux / lipid droplet / lysosomal degradation)
DAM2_markers_confirmed: LAM defined by lipid-clearing signature; specific canonical DAM-2 markers (CST7/CLEC7A) not primary focus

notes_for_DAM_DRUG: NEW TF TARGETS — PLAN AMENDMENT REQUIRED. BHLHE40 and BHLHE41 are basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) transcriptional REPRESSORS of the LAM/DLAM state in human and mouse microglia. AD GWAS enrichment confirmed: AD risk alleles enriched in BHLHE40/41 cistromes (stratified LD-score regression). THERAPEUTIC DIRECTION: BHLHE40/41 INHIBITION increases lipid-clearing capacity — KO of BHLHE40/41 → increased efferocytosis + cholesterol efflux + lysosomal degradation. This is COMPLEMENTARY to LDAM strategy (Haney 2024): inhibiting BHLHE40/41 would theoretically help microglia handle the toxic lipid burden seen in LDAM. GRN TOOL: ARACNE (not pySCENIC) + ATAC-seq motif enrichment — independent GRN method; orthogonal to pySCENIC validation. FUNCTIONAL VALIDATION: KO validated in iPSC-derived human microglia + THP-1 macrophages + in vivo mouse microglia — strong multi-model evidence. DRUGGABILITY: No approved BHLHE40/41 inhibitors (Finan Tier 3). BHLHE40/41 are bHLH TFs — dimerization-dependent DNA binding → PPI inhibitor strategy at bHLH dimerization interface is the most tractable approach. Published Nat Commun March 2024 (DOI: 10.1038/s41467-024-46315-7). PLAN ACTION: Add BHLHE40/BHLHE41 to secondary TF target list; cross-reference with pySCENIC regulon output in Phase 2; investigate bHLH interface as docking target in Phase 3.
